varieties.name: Kwai Mai Pink
varieties.origin: China (Guangdong province), ancient cultivar with 500-600 year history. Introduced to Australia in 1930s.
varieties.traits: Round fruit, red skin with slight orange hue. Medium size, small to medium seed. Distinctive dark green/black tubercle tips near fruit stalk.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Spicy-sweet flavor, firm flesh, excellent quality. Small seed with high flesh recovery.

varieties.name: Wai Chee
varieties.origin: China (Guangdong province), 500-600 year heritage. Means 'cherished lychee'.
varieties.traits: Heart-shaped to round fruit. Deep red skin with thick, irregular segments and smooth protuberances. Small, oval leaflets that curve upwards.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet and juicy, watery texture when fully ripe. Medium sized fruit, flat shoulders.
varieties.yield_notes: Very consistent bearer, reliable annual crops with good alternating year yields.
varieties.availability: Available at Queensland nurseries including Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery.

varieties.name: Tai So
varieties.origin: China, ancient cultivar. First introduced to Hawaii in 1873, to Australia in 1930s.
varieties.traits: Egg-shaped fruit, distinctive spearhead/chicken-heart shape. Red skin with hairlike sharp protuberances. Large, glossy dark green leaves with upward curl.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet and aromatic with pleasant flavor. Variable chicken-tongue seed percentage depending on season.
varieties.yield_notes: Irregular bearing, good crops every 2-3 years. Can produce 10-50kg per 10-year-old tree.

varieties.name: Bengal
varieties.origin: India (historical variety from Bengal region), introduced to Australia in late 1800s.
varieties.traits: Bright red skin, round to heart-shaped fruit. Large leaflets, mid-green with distinctive twist along length. Sharp-pointed protuberances. Uneven shoulders. Large, bold pointed seed.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet with exceptional rose-like fragrance. Classic lychee flavor profile.
varieties.yield_notes: Irregular growing habit affects production. Rarely produces chicken-tongue seeds.

varieties.name: Salathiel
varieties.origin: Australia (developed in Australia, surname of developer), introduced within last 50 years.
varieties.traits: Bright red with smooth skin, slightly lopsided round shape. Small fruit (approximately 30-32mm diameter). Weak vigor, compact tree.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very high proportion of small/aborted seeds (near-seedless). Excellent flesh-to-seed ratio. Sweet flavor, good quality.
varieties.availability: Available at Queensland nurseries including Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery.

varieties.name: Souey Tung
varieties.origin: China (Fujian province), relatively young cultivar (approximately 100 years old). First variety cultivated in Australia.
varieties.traits: Large fruit, red to orange-red skin. Thin skin with obvious suture line. Uneven shoulders. Dominates Fujian province plantings.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flavor and quality. Produces some chicken-tongue seeds.

varieties.name: Baitaying (Baitangying)
varieties.origin: China (Guangdong province), traditional cultivar with 27,000 hectares under cultivation in Guangdong.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit, red to orange color. Can be stored well for extended periods.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality flesh with good flavor.

varieties.name: Chom Pogo (Chompogo)
varieties.origin: Chinese origin, now grown in Australia.
varieties.traits: Medium to large red fruit on dome-shaped tree. Fast-growing tree structure.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Relatively small seed, good flesh recovery. Quality fruit.

varieties.name: Erdon Lee
varieties.origin: Chinese origin, modern introduction to Australia.
varieties.traits: EXTRA-LARGE fruit - up to 4 times average size (52g average weight, some fruits 60-80g). Dark greenish and red exterior. Rough, wrinkled skin.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptional quality, super-sized flesh. Dark red color when mature.

varieties.name: Fay Zee Siu
varieties.origin: China (Guangdong province), traditional cultivar with 27,000 hectares in Guangdong. Recently imported to Australia.
varieties.traits: Large fruit (24-32g), green speckled skin effect. Red skin with distinctive appearance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent eating quality, very sweet taste. Highly regarded in China.
varieties.availability: Increasingly available at Queensland nurseries as plantings expand.

varieties.name: Sah Keng
varieties.origin: Taiwan (developed within last 50 years), Thai variety.
varieties.traits: Large red fruit with smallish seed. Good fruit size.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good eating quality, sweet flavor.

varieties.name: Bosworth 3
varieties.origin: Same as Kwai Mai Pink - ancient Chinese cultivar.

varieties.name: Shahi (Rose Scented)
varieties.origin: Muzaffarpur, Bihar, India. Traditional Indian variety with GI (Geographical Indication) tag granted 2018. Indigenous to North India, adapted to South India.
varieties.traits: Large fruits (20-25g), thin smooth skin with very distinct protuberances. Boat-shaped leaves. Light-colored vegetative flush. Long panicles.
varieties.flesh_fruit: High quality with distinctive ROSE AROMA (characteristic fragrance). Juicy pulp, pleasant sugar-acid balance. Highest pulp percentage. Aromatic pulp with rose-like fragrance.
varieties.yield_notes: Very vigorous trees, 100-150kg per mature plant. Excellent productivity.

varieties.name: China
varieties.origin: Chinese origin, adapted and naturalized in India over centuries. Major variety in Bihar and West Bengal.
varieties.traits: Large size fruit with high pulp content. Thick skin. Leaves have distinctive twist along length, curved upward from midrib. Pinkish vegetative flush. Pointed apex.
varieties.flesh_fruit: High pulp content, excellent quality. Bold, fully-developed seeds (not chicken-tongue type).
varieties.yield_notes: HIGH yield - very commercially profitable. Popular with farmers for productivity.

varieties.name: Early Seedless
varieties.origin: Developed in India (Bihar/Uttar Pradesh region). Indian selection for tropical conditions.
varieties.traits: Late-maturing variety with nearly seedless fruit. Extended shelf life making it suitable for distant markets.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Nearly seedless with very high flesh recovery. Good quality, sweet flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable production. Among highest yields in Indian trials (12-130kg per tree).

varieties.name: Dehradun
varieties.origin: Dehradun, Uttarakhand, India. Traditional Himalayan foothill variety with centuries of cultivation.
varieties.traits: Famous rose-scented variety from Dehradun region. Good size fruit.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Known for sweetness and abundance of juice. Aromatic with good flavor profile.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity. Major commercial variety in Uttarakhand (18,700 metric tons annually from Dehradun/Haridwar region).
varieties.availability: Available from Kerala nurseries and South Indian suppliers.

varieties.name: Calcutta (Kalkattia)
varieties.origin: Calcutta (Kolkata), West Bengal, India. Traditional Bengali variety.
varieties.traits: Vibrant red skin, distinctive appearance. Well-known in traditional markets.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet flavor, good quality. High pulp content.
varieties.yield_notes: Among highest-yielding varieties in Indian trials (12-130kg per tree, second only to Calcutta variety in yields).
varieties.availability: Available from Kerala and West Bengal nurseries.

varieties.name: Maclean (McLean's Red)
varieties.origin: Indian selection, adapted variety.
varieties.traits: Red fruit with good characteristics.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality flesh with pleasant flavor.

varieties.name: Bedana
varieties.origin: Indian variety, traditional cultivation.
varieties.traits: Very thick skin. Small, oval-shaped leaflets. Very smooth protuberances. Short, compact panicles. Very dark pink vegetative flush.
varieties.flesh_fruit: HIGH proportion of chicken-tongued seeds (aborted seeds = more edible flesh). Excellent flesh recovery.

varieties.name: Bombay
varieties.origin: Maharashtra, India (Bombay/Mumbai region).
varieties.traits: Large size fruit with distinctive characteristics.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very juicy pulp, sweet flavor. Good quality.

varieties.name: Longia
varieties.origin: Bihar, India (Muzaffarpur region). Local traditional variety.
varieties.traits: Local variety characteristics adapted to Bihar conditions.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, traditional flavor profile valued locally.

varieties.name: Purbi
varieties.origin: Bihar, India (Muzaffarpur region). Local traditional variety.
varieties.traits: Local variety characteristics.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality suitable for local markets.

varieties.name: Gulabi
varieties.origin: Bihar and West Bengal, India. Traditional variety.
varieties.traits: Pinkish hue to skin, distinctive coloration. Fragrant aroma.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with pleasant fragrance. Good quality.
varieties.availability: Available from Bihar and West Bengal nurseries.

varieties.name: Swarna Roopa
varieties.origin: Developed by Central Horticultural Experiment Station (CHES), Ranchi, Jharkhand, India. Modern Indian breeding selection.
varieties.traits: Selected for specific improved characteristics including crack resistance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: High TSS (Total Soluble Solids) content. Small seeds. Excellent quality.
varieties.availability: Available from CHES Ranchi and Jharkhand nurseries.

varieties.name: Green
varieties.origin: India, traditional variety.
varieties.traits: Distinctive green characteristics maintained longer.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality when fully mature.

varieties.name: Early Large Red
varieties.origin: India, developed variety.
varieties.traits: Large fruit size with bright red coloration. Early maturity.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, sweet flavor.
